pain_management.title: Managing Pain at Home
pain_management.content:
Pain management strategies include:

• Proper positioning - Use pillows for support
• Heat/cold therapy - Apply as directed by your care team
• Relaxation techniques - Deep breathing, meditation
• Medication adherence - Take medications as prescribed
• Gentle movement - Light stretching if approved

Always consult your care team before making changes to your pain management plan.

pain_management.title: When to Call Your Care Team
pain_management.content:
Contact your hospice care team immediately if:

• Pain suddenly worsens or changes
• Current pain medication isn't working
• New pain appears in a different location
• Pain prevents sleep or basic activities
• You experience side effects from pain medication

Your comfort is the priority - never hesitate to reach out.

nausea_relief.title: Coping with Nausea
nausea_relief.content:
Try these approaches:

• Small, frequent meals instead of large ones
• Avoid strong odors and greasy foods
• Stay hydrated with clear fluids
• Rest after eating
• Ginger tea may help (check with your care team)
• Keep the room well-ventilated
• Eat bland foods like crackers or toast

If nausea persists or worsens, contact your care team about anti-nausea medications.

anxiety_coping.title: Managing Anxiety
anxiety_coping.content:
Anxiety is common and manageable:

• Deep breathing exercises - Breathe in for 4, hold for 4, out for 4
• Meditation or guided imagery
• Gentle music or nature sounds
• Talk with loved ones about your feelings
• Keep a journal
• Focus on what you can control
• Ask about anxiety medications if needed

Don't hesitate to ask for professional support - it's a sign of strength, not weakness.

breathing_techniques.title: Breathing Exercises
breathing_techniques.content:
These techniques can help:

• Sit upright with good posture
• Use a fan for air circulation
• Pursed-lip breathing: Breathe in through nose, out slowly through pursed lips
• Diaphragmatic breathing: Place hand on belly, breathe deeply so belly rises
• Keep the room cool and well-ventilated
• Use oxygen as prescribed

Contact your care team immediately if breathing becomes severely difficult.

family_communication.title: Talking with Family
family_communication.content:
Open communication helps everyone:

• Share your feelings honestly
• Ask questions - there are no silly questions
• Listen actively to others
• It's okay to have difficult conversations
• Express your wishes and preferences
• Allow others to express their emotions
• Consider family meetings with your care team

Your care team can facilitate family discussions if needed.

emergency_contacts.title: Emergency Contacts
emergency_contacts.content:
Keep these numbers accessible:

• Hospice 24/7 Nurse Line: [Contact your provider]
• Your Primary Hospice Nurse: [Contact your provider]
• Hospice Social Worker: [Contact your provider]
• Chaplain Services: [Contact your provider]
• Emergency Services: 911

For non-emergency questions, call during business hours. For urgent concerns, the 24/7 line is always available.
